---
title: "Ship Faster, Break Less"
subtitle: "5 Practices That Changed How We Deploy"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: ../themes/impeccable-lightning.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    hash: true
    controls: true
    progress: true
    center: false
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    auto-animate: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    highlight-style: github-dark
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@100..900&family=Source+Sans+3:ital,wght@0,200..900;1,200..900&family=JetBrains+Mono:ital,wght@0,100..800;1,100..800&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
---

## The Problem

::: {.impact}
::: {.impact-number}
47min
:::
::: {.impact-label}
Average time from commit to production
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Hit them with the number immediately. No warm-up in a lightning talk.
47 minutes is slow. Everyone in the audience knows it.
:::

---

## We Got It Down To This

::: {.impact}
::: {.impact-number}
4min
:::
::: {.impact-label}
Same codebase. Same team. Five changes.
:::
:::

---

## Practice 1

### Trunk-Based Development

::: {.keybox}
**One rule:** No branch lives longer than 24 hours.

Small changes, merged fast, deployed continuously.
:::

- Feature flags replace long-lived branches
- PRs are [**< 200 lines**]{.hi}, always
- Review turnaround target: [**< 2 hours**]{.hi-gold}

::: {.notes}
This is the hardest habit change. Engineers love their feature branches.
The key insight: small PRs get reviewed faster, merged faster, and break less.
:::

---

## Practice 2

### Parallel Testing

:::: {.comparison}
::: {.compare-left}
### Before

Tests run sequentially

**18 minutes** to get results

Team waits, context-switches
:::

::: {.compare-right}
### After

Tests run in parallel shards

**3 minutes** to get results

Flow state preserved
:::
::::

---

## Practice 3

### Progressive Rollouts

```yaml
# deploy.yml — every deploy goes through gates
rollout:
  - stage: canary
    percent: 1
    duration: 5m
    gates: [error_rate < 0.1%, latency_p99 < 200ms]
  - stage: regional
    percent: 25
    duration: 10m
    gates: [error_rate < 0.5%, latency_p99 < 300ms]
  - stage: global
    percent: 100
```

::: {.tipbox}
**Result:** Zero customer-impacting incidents from deployments in 6 months.
:::

---

## Practice 4

### Automated Rollback

::: {.impact}
::: {.impact-statement}
If metrics breach thresholds,
[rollback happens automatically]{.hi}
in under 90 seconds.
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Humans are too slow to detect and respond to production issues.
Automated rollback removed the fear from deploying.
:::

---

## Practice 5

### Deploy on Friday

::: {.warningbox}
**Controversial Take**

If you're afraid to deploy on Friday, your deployment process is broken — not your schedule.
:::

When you have:

- [Trunk-based development]{.hi}
- [Fast parallel tests]{.hi-gold}
- [Progressive rollouts]{.hi-green}
- [Automated rollback]{.hi}

...every day is a good day to deploy.

---

## The Results

:::: {.two-col}
::: {.div}
### Deploy Speed

- **47min → 4min** deploy time
- **3x/day → 25x/day** frequency
- **0** deployment-caused incidents (6mo)
:::

::: {.div}
### Team Impact

- **60% less** time in CI queues
- **No more** deployment freezes
- Engineers deploy [**their own code**]{.hi}
:::
::::

---

## One Slide Summary

::: {.keybox}
**Five Practices**

1. **Trunk-based:** No branch > 24 hours
2. **Parallel tests:** 18min → 3min
3. **Progressive rollout:** Canary → Regional → Global
4. **Auto rollback:** < 90 seconds, no humans
5. **Deploy fearlessly:** Every day, including Friday
:::

[Thank you!]{.larger}

::: {.notes}
Lightning talk — no Q&A time. Point people to your blog/repo for details.
End right on the 5-minute mark. Respect the format.
:::
